focus: Which frontmatter schema does the installed Claude runtime enforce? RESOLVED: Claude=tools: string, OpenCode=permission: object; create-agent emits only permission: for both (wrong for Claude); .claude/agents/deep-improvement.md uses wrong schema (permission: instead of tools:)

label: Claude enforces tools: string; OpenCode enforces permission: object; create-agent emits only permission: for both runtimes
evidence: 11/12 .claude/agents use tools: string; 12/12 .opencode/agents use permission: object; create-agent SKILL.md:71-96 declares permission: as sole canonical; line 105 and 177 label tools: as deprecated

label: deep-improvement.md Claude copy uses OpenCode permission: schema instead of Claude tools: — no tool enforcement under Claude Code
evidence: Uses mode/temperature/permission: object; no tools: field; all other 11 .claude/agents use tools:; Claude ignores permission: block → agent inherits full parent toolset

label: create-agent/SKILL.md falsely labels tools: as deprecated — it is the active canonical Claude schema
evidence: Line 105: 'do not use deprecated standalone tools:'; line 177: 'Never use deprecated standalone tools:' — tools: is NOT deprecated; it is the only enforceable schema under Claude Code

label: validate_document.py has no agent frontmatter schema enforcement
evidence: 838-line validator; grep for permission/tools/frontmatter returns no schema-checking hits; validates markdown structure only

label: deep-improvement mirror pair has identical frontmatter — Claude copy is verbatim of OpenCode original, root cause of wrong-schema bug

direction: permission: as universal canonical for Claude
reason: Claude Code runtime ignores permission: object entirely — only tools: string is enforced; confirmed by 11/12 existing .claude/agents using tools:
